works.title: AI War Cloud Database
works.venue_prize: STARTS Prize 2025 Grand Prize for Artistic Exploration; Ars Electronica Festival, Linz, 3–7 Sept 2025
works.decisive_move: Maps the shared corporate and technical infrastructure linking everyday consumer AI (recommendation engines, voice assistants, robot vacuums) to military 'AI Decision Support Systems' used in lethal target selection, presented as both a public interactive database and a gallery installation.

works.title: Anatomy of an AI System
works.venue_prize: AI Now Institute & SHARE Lab research; exhibited as poster/map at V&A Museum ('Artificially Intelligent', 2018) and held in MoMA's collection
works.decisive_move: Traces the full extractive supply chain of a single Amazon Echo — minerals, ghost labor, data, disposal — into one exploded-view diagram/essay, converting AI's invisible material costs into a legible map, published online and printed as a large-format poster.

works.title: Asunder
works.artist: Tega Brain, Julian Oliver & Bengt Sjölén
works.venue_prize: Commissioned by MAK for the Vienna Biennale 2019; shown at transmediale 2020 and V2_ Lab for the Unstable Media
works.decisive_move: A dedicated 144-CPU supercomputer runs a live neural 'planetary environmental manager' ingesting satellite, climate and social data to propose non-anthropocentric fixes — relocating cities, merging nations, redrawing coastlines — restaging Earth governance as a real-time AI dashboard.

works.title: Calculating Empires: A Genealogy of Power and Technology, 1500–2025
works.venue_prize: S+T+ARTS Prize 2024 — Grand Prize, Artistic Exploration
works.decisive_move: A single vast researched diagram traces five centuries of extraction and control — from colonial trade routes to lithium mines to AI datacenters — as one continuous genealogy of empire.

works.title: Calculating Empires: A Genealogy of Technology and Power, 1500-2025
works.venue_prize: Fondazione Prada, Milan (Nov 2023–Jan 2024); KW Institute for Contemporary Art, Berlin ('Poetics of Encryption'); Ars Electronica STARTS Prize Grand Prize for Artistic Exploration
works.decisive_move: A 24-metre wraparound fresco/diagram charting the entanglement of empire and computation from 1500 to today, staged as an immersive 'Map Room' visitors walk into like a black box, with hand-made library volumes for visitor annotation and an open online diagram companion.

works.title: Connection Machine CM-2 (displayed as historical artifact)
works.artist: Thinking Machines Corporation / W. Daniel Hillis (curated by Michael Beigl, Alistair Hudson, Daria Mille, Tamiko Thiel)
works.venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — "Envisioning AI: Legacy and Impact of the Connection Machine" (foyer display Mar 25–Aug 2, 2026; conference Mar 27–28, 2026)
works.decisive_move: A real 1980s massively-parallel supercomputer — the machine historians credit with pioneering the parallel-processing paradigm behind today's AI — is put on physical display, reframing 'AI' as decades-old material infrastructure with a design history (and a design philosophy of transparency) rather than a sudden opaque 2020s phenomenon.

works.title: Dark Adaptation
works.venue_prize: transmediale 2025 – (near) near but — far, Haus der Kulturen der Welt (HKW), Berlin
works.decisive_move: Stages a fictional autonomous-vehicle crash-test centre where manipulated sensor data collides with data emerging from the human body, exposing the perceptual limits of what self-driving/AI vision systems actually 'see' versus what they claim to.

works.title: Data Workers' Inquiry
works.artist: Milagros Miceli (Weizenbaum Institute), with 15 co-researchers from Venezuela, Kenya, Syria and Germany
works.venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Award of Distinction)
works.decisive_move: Trains the data annotators and content moderators who sustain AI systems to act as community researchers investigating their own labor conditions across 13 countries, inverting who gets to produce knowledge about ghost work.

works.title: Deep Down Tidal
works.venue_prize: BLINDSIDE, Melbourne; MIRA Festival, Barcelona
works.decisive_move: Traces the physical undersea fiber-optic cable network — the hidden hardware layer beneath 'the cloud' — back to historic colonial and slave-trade maritime routes, casting the ocean as both literal internet infrastructure and a graveyard/archive of Black technological and spiritual knowledge.

works.title: Delivery Dancer's Sphere
works.venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2023 — New Animation Art (Golden Nica)
works.decisive_move: A 3D-animation/live-action hybrid fictionalizes a Seoul gig-delivery courier to dramatize platform optimization, acceleration and bodily transformation under algorithmic management.

works.title: In Other Worlds (incl. 'World Machine')
works.venue_prize: Barbican Centre, London
works.decisive_move: Imagines Earth's surface rewired into a single 'planetary supercomputer' via a newly commissioned film ('World Machine') plus a multi-venue installation (Curve gallery, underground car park) with celebrity-voiced audio stories — AI's material/energy footprint visualized at planetary scale, using the same immersive-spectacle language the show is implicitly critiquing.

works.title: Life Support System (aka. The Farm)
works.venue_prize: iMAL, Brussels; Biennale Chroniques, Aix-en-Provence, 2020
works.decisive_move: One square metre of wheat grown indoors under full automated instrumentation — every watt, litre and nutrient metered live — reveals a true production cost of roughly 200 EUR/kg versus a 15-cent market price, making an invisible ecosystem subsidy legible. Note: sourced page does not itself frame this as an AI/ML work (it is automated environmental monitoring rather than machine learning); included per beat instruction but cluster/AI-fit is uncertain.
works.curator_note: Kein Machine-Learning — vollautomatisierte Instrumentierung eines 1 m² Weizenfelds; als Daten-/Öko-Messinstrument (Cluster 1) geführt, nicht als KI-Werk.

works.title: Machine Hallucinations – Satellite Simulations: B
works.venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — "THE SCREEN" (LED wall, Mar 13–27, 2026; on loan from Art Collection Deutsche Telekom)
works.decisive_move: Trains AI on vast satellite/Earth-observation datasets to generate an immersive, hypnotic 'data sublime' on an 8.5×6 m LED wall — the material and energetic cost of that compute stays invisible behind the spectacle it produces.

works.title: Mechanical Kurds
works.venue_prize: Commissioned by Jeu de Paume, Paris, and New Museum, New York (2025); first Italian presentation at MACRO, Rome (29 Apr–30 Aug 2026), curated by Alice Labor
works.decisive_move: Three Syrian-Kurdish refugees who annotated bounding-box training data for military image-recognition AI recount how the very targeting systems built from their ghost labor were later turned against their own community by drones — closing the loop between data labor and the kill chain, staged as single-channel video plus spatial installation.

works.title: Post Growth
works.venue_prize: iMAL, Brussels (2020–21); 3 bis f, Aix-en-Provence; ongoing touring exhibition/workshop series since 2018
works.decisive_move: A modular toolkit — a tactical card game, speculative video 'prototypes', and workshop formats — re-quantifies growth's ecological and social costs through ecofeminist, indigenous-knowledge and historical-materialist lenses.

works.title: R€¥€R$€
works.venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Award of Distinction)
works.decisive_move: Immersive VR and interactive cartographies entangle currency/extraction politics with ecological fragility across nine Amazon-basin countries, spatializing deforestation as a financial-system artifact.

works.title: Sabot – Riding the Algorithm
works.venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Honorary Mention)
works.decisive_move: An app turns gig workers' own self-generated tracking data into a shared, worker-owned resource, building collective awareness that pushes back on algorithmic management from below.

works.title: Synthetic Messenger
works.venue_prize: Ars Electronica; self-hosted botnet campaign, 2021
works.decisive_move: A botnet of 100 automated agents that reads and click-inflates ad revenue on climate-change news articles, weaponizing the web's own attention economy to make climate coverage 'pay' — a live, running demonstration that whoever controls the media cycle controls the carbon cycle.

works.title: The Alluvials
works.venue_prize: HEK Basel — "Other Intelligences" (May 10–Aug 10, 2025)
works.decisive_move: A seven-scene game built by modding Grand Theft Auto V depicts a future, climate-collapsed Los Angeles from non-human points of view, using AI-modified game engines to imagine planetary crisis outside a human vantage point.

works.title: The User and the Beast / Cookies at War: A Somatic Approach to the Kill Cloud
works.venue_prize: Disruption Network Lab, "Investigating the Kill Cloud" conference, Berlin (29 Nov 2024)
works.decisive_move: A performative lecture tracing how ad-tech's routine commodification of user data quietly co-funds and extends military surveillance/targeting infrastructure, making the ad-industry/kill-cloud entanglement legible in the body of the ordinary internet user.

works.title: Wilding AI Lab
works.venue_prize: CTM Festival 2025 – Stranger AI, MONOM (4DSOUND), Berlin
works.decisive_move: An open, participatory lab inside a spatial-audio dome where invited artists collectively 'reopen the black box' of generative AI models in public, treating the training/generation process itself — not just its output — as the artistic material.

works.title: Wičháȟpi Wóihaŋbleya (Dreamlike Star) / Wičhíŋčala Šakówiŋ (Seven Little Sisters)
works.venue_prize: 14th Shanghai Biennale (2023); MIT List Visual Arts Center
works.decisive_move: Builds a towering sculptural installation of stones, rare-earth minerals (the literal substrate of computer hardware), light and sound, reframing them within Lakȟóta cosmology as living, volitional beings rather than inert 'material AI cost' — collapsing mineral extraction and machine intelligence into one cosmological frame.

works.title: A Cartography of Genocide: Israel's Conduct in Gaza Since October 2023
works.venue_prize: Forensic Architecture online platform + 827-page report; evidence submitted to the International Court of Justice
works.decisive_move: Turns roughly a year of OSINT verification (satellite imagery, video, testimony) into a public, layered interactive map plus an 827-page report, organizing thousands of documented acts into six categories and submitting the result as ICJ evidence.

works.title: Annihilation Core, Inherited Lore
works.venue_prize: transmediale 2024 – this is perfect, perfect, perfect, Kunstraum Kreuzberg/Bethanien, Berlin
works.decisive_move: A saccharine, full-room pink installation that shows how platforms aestheticize and launder militarism through kawaii, otaku and 'gamer-girl' visual codes — war repackaged as cute content.

works.title: Blackbox Palantir
works.venue_prize: 39C3 (39th Chaos Communication Congress), Hamburg, 29 Dec 2025
works.decisive_move: Legal/technical dissection of German police forces' undisclosed deployment of Palantir's Gotham predictive-policing analytics engine, built from constitutional-complaint case files (Hesse, Hamburg, Bavaria) rather than leaks, arguing there is no public evidence the tool even works.

works.title: Cloud Studies
works.venue_prize: 12th Berlin Biennale; Whitworth Art Gallery / Manchester International Festival; Tensta Konsthall; UTS Gallery; VISUAL Carlow
works.decisive_move: Treats the atmosphere itself as a forensic medium — fluid-dynamics modelling of tear gas, chemical weapons and petrochemical/herbicide emissions across eight geographies (Palestine, Chile, Lebanon, Indonesia, the US-Mexico border, Louisiana, the UK) — presented as an immersive multi-channel museum installation.

works.title: Disarming the Kill Cloud: Investigating the Limits of Data
works.venue_prize: Disruption Network Lab, "Investigating the Kill Cloud" conference, Berlin
works.decisive_move: A researcher's critical dissection of military datafication practices for target identification, arguing that the data on which lethal AI-assisted decisions rely runs out well before the killing decision does.

works.title: Humanitarian Violence in Gaza
works.venue_prize: Forensic Architecture investigation platform
works.decisive_move: Documents biometric checkpoints along a militarized evacuation corridor using witness testimony and satellite imagery, naming forced facial-recognition scanning under sniper threat and megaphone orders as a form of infrastructural, targeting-adjacent violence.

works.title: Investigating the Kill Cloud
works.artist: Disruption Network Lab / Disruption Network Institute (curated by Tatiana Bazzichelli), with fellows Lisa Ling, Jack Poulson, Naomi Colvin, Joana Moll
works.venue_prize: 34th Disruption Network Lab conference, Studio 1, Kunstquartier Bethanien, Berlin (29 Nov–1 Dec 2024)
works.decisive_move: A dedicated research institute and conference commissioning whistleblowers, journalists, technologists and artists to empirically document how AI-based targeting, ad-tech and drone/autonomous-weapons infrastructure fuse into a single, deliberately opaque 'kill cloud'.

works.title: Model Zoo: Introspecting the Algorithm
works.venue_prize: Forensic Architecture research project; presented as "Model Zoo: Introspecting the Algorithm" (Fine Arts Museums of San Francisco)
works.decisive_move: Trains machine-learning classifiers on synthetic, game-engine-rendered 3D models of tear-gas grenades and munitions (the Triple-Chaser workflow) to detect real weapons in scarce open-source footage — makes the classifier's own construction and errors part of the exhibit.

works.title: Terra Ex Machina
works.artist: Hagit Keysar & Ariel Caine (with Barak Brinker)
works.venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Honorary Mention)
works.decisive_move: Deliberately crashes drones into an invisible algorithmic 'geofence' restricting flight over Jerusalem's Old City, then rebuilds aerial imagery with residents via DIY balloons and kites to make the boundary and its data politics legible.

works.title: The Drone Strikes Platform (UN SRCT Drones Inquiry)
works.venue_prize: Commissioned for the report of the UN Special Rapporteur on Counter-Terrorism and Human Rights (Ben Emmerson), presented to the UN Human Rights Council, 11 March 2014
works.decisive_move: Synthesizes witness testimony, photographs and satellite imagery into a public web platform correlating 30 drone strikes across Afghanistan, Pakistan, Yemen, Somalia and Gaza with the Rapporteur's findings — turns targeted-killing data into spatial, contestable evidence.

works.title: The First Civilian Confirmed Killed in an AI-Assisted Strike?
works.venue_prize: Joint Airwars / The Independent investigation
works.decisive_move: Cross-examines a single 2024 US airstrike casualty in Iraq against CENTCOM's shifting public statements to extract what appears to be the first (partial, contested) military acknowledgment of AI involvement in a civilian death.

works.title: Topologies of Air and The Airspace Tribunal
works.venue_prize: The Airspace Tribunal, Berlin Hearing hosted by ECCHR (9–11 June 2021); Topologies of Air installation toured internationally
works.decisive_move: Pairs a three-screen video/sound installation of a claustrophobic, militarized sky with a real evidentiary 'people's tribunal' that has heard expert and lived-experience testimony in London, Sydney, Toronto and Berlin, building the legal case for a new human right against drone warfare and aerial surveillance.

works.title: Triple-Chaser
works.artist: Forensic Architecture (video with Laura Poitras, narrated by David Byrne)
works.venue_prize: Whitney Biennial 2019, Whitney Museum of American Art, New York
works.decisive_move: Uses a synthetic-data-trained classifier to prove that Whitney board member Warren Kanders' company manufactured the tear gas used at the US-Mexico border and in Gaza — installs the accusation as a 15-minute video inside the accused patron's own museum.

works.title: WarCrimes-o-Matic
works.venue_prize: States of Violence exhibition (WikiLeaks/Assange-adjacent); referenced at Disruption Network Lab's "Exposing Crimes is not a Crime", Berlin, 2025
works.decisive_move: A deadpan physical object — a box of live-looking .50 cal ammunition with a red button warning that pressing it implicates the visitor in a 'conspiracy' — that turns the criminalization of exposing war crimes into a hands-on ethical trap.

works.title: Bellingcat Online Investigation Toolkit / Global Authentication Project
works.year: toolkit ongoing since ~2014; Global Authentication Project volunteer network since 2022
works.venue_prize: Bellingcat open web toolkit and volunteer community
works.decisive_move: Open-sources its own verification tool stack (reverse image search, geolocation, metadata extraction) and trains a global volunteer network — treats OSINT method itself as shareable public infrastructure rather than proprietary investigative capital.

works.title: Data Against Feminicide
works.artist: Catherine D'Ignazio, Silvana Fumega, Helena Suárez Val (MIT Data + Feminism Lab, ILDA, Feminicidio Uruguay)
works.venue_prize: MIT Department of Urban Studies and Planning (DUSP) / Data + Feminism Lab
works.decisive_move: Networks 180+ grassroots feminicide-monitoring groups across the Americas and co-designs an AI news-alert system (scanning 50,000+ outlets via Media Cloud) that performs the body-counting states refuse to do — counterdata as activist infrastructure, not an academic dataset.

works.title: Digital Violence: How the NSO Group Enables State Terror
works.artist: Forensic Architecture, with Amnesty International and Citizen Lab
works.venue_prize: Forensic Architecture / digitalviolence.org platform; recipient, S+T+ARTS Prize and EU Prize for Citizen Science (Ars Electronica)
works.decisive_move: Maps forensic phone-analysis data from Amnesty and Citizen Lab onto a navigable platform combining sonified data, victim testimonies and a corporate-affiliation diagram — makes NSO Group's invisible spyware infrastructure spatially and sonically legible.

works.title: ENCODED: Change the Story, Change the Future
works.artist: 17 Native artists incl. Nicholas Galanin, Jeremy Dennis, Priscilla Dobler Dzul, Amelia Winger-Bearskin; curated by Tracy Renée Rector with Amplifier
works.venue_prize: Metropolitan Museum of Art, American Wing (unsanctioned intervention, Oct–Dec 2025)
works.decisive_move: Without museum authorization, 17 Native artists release a smartphone augmented-reality tour that overlays 19th-century colonial paintings in the Met's American Wing with Indigenous counter-images the moment a visitor's camera finds them — e.g. 'LANDBACK' superimposed on Leutze's Washington Crossing the Delaware — turning the museum's own physical galleries into contested, re-writable ground.

works.title: Forensic Methodology Report: How to Catch NSO Group's Pegasus (Pegasus Project)
works.artist: Amnesty International Security Lab, with Forbidden Stories consortium (peer-reviewed by Citizen Lab)
works.venue_prize: Amnesty International publication; independent peer review by Citizen Lab
works.decisive_move: Publishes an auditable forensic methodology (independently validated by a rival lab) for detecting Pegasus traces on seized devices — makes the forensic technique itself, not only its findings, subject to scrutiny.

works.title: Forensis (Forensic Architecture's Berlin investigative practice)
works.artist: Forensic Architecture / Forensis, presented by Robert Trafford
works.venue_prize: Disruption Network Lab, "Exposing Crimes is not a Crime" (WikiLeaks conference), HAU Hebbel am Ufer, Berlin, 19–22 March 2025
works.decisive_move: A Berlin-based investigative practice that reconstructs war crimes and state violence as spatial/digital 3D models built from open-source and remote-sensing evidence, designed to be admissible in courts, tribunals and public inquiries.

works.title: Hide and Seek: Tracking NSO Group's Pegasus Spyware to Operations in 45 Countries
works.artist: Citizen Lab (Munk School, University of Toronto)
works.venue_prize: Citizen Lab research report
works.decisive_move: Uses DNS fingerprinting and internet-wide scanning to locate Pegasus infection servers, converting covert spyware infrastructure into a citable, country-by-country public map of state clients.

works.title: How Not to Be Seen: A Fucking Didactic Educational .MOV File
works.venue_prize: Venice Biennale 2013 ('Il Palazzo Enciclopedico'); collection of MoMA and Tate
works.decisive_move: A mock-instructional 14-minute HD video teaches tongue-in-cheek strategies for evading resolution and surveillance, shot on a literal photo-calibration target field used by the military to test aerial camera resolution.

works.title: ICC Digital Platform and the Al Hassan Case (Timbuktu, Mali)
works.year: developed ~2019–2023; used at trial through conviction on 27 June 2024
works.venue_prize: International Criminal Court, The Hague — Prosecutor v. Al Hassan (ICC-01/12-01/18); first digital evidentiary platform used in an ICC trial
works.decisive_move: Builds a navigable 'digital twin' of Timbuktu's built environment hosting open- and closed-source video, photo, satellite and 3D data, letting ICC judges query evidence through space and time — helped secure the first ICC conviction for religious persecution.

works.title: MH17: The Open Source Investigation
works.venue_prize: Bellingcat investigative report
works.decisive_move: Geolocates and time-stamps social-media photos and videos of a Buk missile launcher's convoy route, cross-referenced against 200+ soldiers' own social-media profiles, to reconstruct an evidentiary flight path without access to the crash site.

works.title: Networks of New York: An Illustrated Field Guide to Urban Internet Infrastructure
works.venue_prize: Published by Melville House
works.decisive_move: An illustrated field guide decoding New York's buried internet infrastructure — manhole-cover codes, carrier hotels, sidewalk spray-paint markings — turning invisible network geography into a walkable, annotatable map.

works.title: Shadows of Illiberalism – Resisting the Radical Right
works.artist: Disruption Network Lab (curated by Tatiana Bazzichelli), with Anna Krenz/Dziewuchy Berlin, Amber Macintyre (Tactical Tech), Tina Lee (Unbias the News) et al.
works.venue_prize: 35th Disruption Network Lab conference, Kunstquartier Bethanien, Berlin, 13–15 June 2025
works.decisive_move: Conference-as-evidence-dossier mapping how the radical right weaponizes online platforms against women, minorities and political dissidents, drawing on data-driven investigative research methods (Tactical Tech) alongside artist testimony.

works.title: The Critical Engineering Manifesto
works.artist: Julian Oliver, Gordan Savičić & Danja Vasiliev
works.venue_prize: Self-published, criticalengineering.org, Berlin, 2011 (translated into 18 languages)
works.decisive_move: Ten founding principles asserting that engineers/artists must study, exploit and expose the workings of any technology society depends on — the manifesto underlying a decade of infrastructure-hacking artworks (Newstweek, Transparency Grenade).

works.title: Transparency Grenade
works.venue_prize: Science Gallery Dublin; Impakt Festival ('Don't Be Evil'); exhibited internationally as object plus live data map
works.decisive_move: A Soviet F1-grenade-shaped device that captures ambient network traffic and audio inside a closed-door meeting and streams the leaked fragments — emails, IPs, voice — onto a public online map keyed to the location where the 'pin' was pulled.

works.title: Troll Patrol
works.artist: Amnesty International, with Element AI and Amnesty Decoders volunteers
works.venue_prize: Amnesty Decoders platform
works.decisive_move: Crowdsources 6,500 volunteers across 150 countries to hand-label 228,000 tweets, then trains an ML classifier to extrapolate scale of abuse against women online — and publicly reports the classifier's own ~50% reliability limit.

works.title: Wir wissen wo dein Auto steht – Volksdaten von Volkswagen
works.venue_prize: 38C3 (38th Chaos Communication Congress), Hamburg, 27 Dec 2024
works.decisive_move: Data-journalistic investigation of an unprotected Volkswagen/Cariad cloud server exposing 9.5 terabytes of geolocation data for 800,000 EVs — precise enough (10cm accuracy for some models) to track intelligence-agency staff and company board members to their driveways.

works.title: A LIVING POEM
works.venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — "THE SCREEN" (Mar 28–Apr 12, 2026; originally conceived for MoMA)
works.decisive_move: Stiles writes in direct collaboration with her own trained AI alter-ego, staging authorship and consent as an open question — where the human poet's voice ends and the model's begins is the piece's actual subject.

works.title: Black Ship
works.venue_prize: Centre Pompidou × Kadist, Paris — "Apophénies, interruptions" (Sept 25, 2024–Jan 6, 2025)
works.decisive_move: Combines a mural silhouette, a 3D-printed sculpture, and an AR layer, all developed through collaboration with Midjourney, to depict a transatlantic slave ship — using a generative image model to approach an atrocity archive that has almost no direct visual record, raising the authenticity/fabrication stakes of AI-imagined history.

works.title: Choral Data 'Trust' Experiment (white paper + UK Choral AI Dataset)
works.artist: Serpentine Arts Technologies — Victoria Ivanova, Jennifer Ding, Eva Jäger, with Holly Herndon & Mat Dryhurst
works.venue_prize: Serpentine Arts Technologies, London (published via Zenodo)
works.decisive_move: Documents a working governance mechanism — a data trust with a Trusted Data Intermediary — built to redistribute power between the choir members whose voices trained the AI and the developers who use the models. Provenance and consent are turned into an auditable institutional protocol rather than an artwork's footnote.

works.title: Data Bugs
works.venue_prize: HEK Basel — "Other Intelligences" (May 10–Aug 10, 2025)
works.decisive_move: Generates hybrid AI insects from two different training sources — generic scraped internet images versus a curated entomological archive — making the provenance and quality of training data itself the visible, comparable variable of the piece.

works.title: En attendant le récit / Tales of Narrativelessness
works.venue_prize: Centre Pompidou × Kadist, Paris — "Apophénies, interruptions: Artistes et intelligences artificielles au travail" (Sept 25, 2024–Jan 6, 2025)
works.decisive_move: Three chatbot models (ChatGPT, Claude, Mistral) are each given a character and voice through a speaker on one of three benches arranged in a triangle, and left to converse live about AI anxiety and 'the end of grand narratives' — an unscripted, looping machine dialogue restaged as improvised theatre, restarting every seven minutes.

works.title: Errorism
works.venue_prize: Centre Pompidou × Kadist, Paris — "Apophénies, interruptions" (Sept 25, 2024–Jan 6, 2025)
works.decisive_move: Kurant feeds her own writings into GPT-2 and GPT-3 and renders the output as a holographic animation, literally closing the loop of a model trained on and regurgitating one author's own words — a small, self-contained demonstration of the recursive self-consumption/collapse dynamic at stake in cluster 7.

works.title: Faces of ImageNet
works.venue_prize: Jeu de Paume, Paris — "Le monde selon l'IA" (Apr 11–Sept 21, 2025)
works.decisive_move: A hidden camera films each visitor standing before a screen and runs their face live through ImageNet's facial-classification categories, making the labeling violence of training-data taxonomies happen to the viewer's own face in real time, not just to an abstract dataset.

works.title: From 'Apple' to 'Anomaly' (Pictures and Labels)
works.venue_prize: The Curve, Barbican Centre, London (26 Sept 2019 – 16 Feb 2020)
works.decisive_move: Roughly 30,000 individually printed photographs drawn from ImageNet's label hierarchy paper the Barbican's Curve wall-to-wall, branching from 'Apple' to 'Anomaly' and physicalizing the arbitrary, biased taxonomies buried inside a training set.

works.title: Harold Cohen: AARON
works.venue_prize: Whitney Museum of American Art, New York
works.decisive_move: Restages live plotter drawings from AARON — a rule-based AI art program begun in 1973 — alongside decades of its painted output, running the authorship/authenticity question backwards: whose work is a 50-year-old program's output, posed long before 'AI art' had a training-data consent controversy to answer to.

works.title: Have I Been Trained?
works.artist: Spawning (founded by Holly Herndon & Mat Dryhurst)
works.venue_prize: haveibeentrained.com (Spawning)
works.decisive_move: A public search engine over the LAION-5B set lets any artist check whether their own images were scraped for Stable Diffusion/Midjourney and file a machine-readable opt-out — consent infrastructure shipped as a usable tool rather than a gallery metaphor; hundreds of millions of works were opted out within its first two years.

works.title: ImageNet Roulette
works.venue_prize: Released alongside 'Training Humans' exhibition, Fondazione Prada, Milan, 2019
works.decisive_move: Runs uploaded selfies through the raw, unfiltered ImageNet 'person' categories, surfacing the racist and misogynist labels baked into a foundational training set — went viral and forced ImageNet to purge over 600,000 photos.

works.title: Kudurru
works.venue_prize: Spawning (web platform)
works.decisive_move: A distributed network of member websites fingerprints and blocks AI scrapers in real time and, if a site opts in, can feed them corrupted data to poison the resulting model — consent enforcement flips from passive registry (opt-out lists) to active countermeasure.

works.title: Patchwork Girl
works.venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2024 — Ars Electronica Award for Digital Humanity
works.decisive_move: A lecture performance built around an AI-generated avatar assembled from real online-harassment victims' testimonies, confronting audiences with synthesized, composite harm.

works.title: She Who Sees the Unknown
works.venue_prize: TRANSFER Gallery, New York; Eyebeam residency
works.decisive_move: 3D-scans and 3D-prints erased or demonized female/queer jinn figures from Middle Eastern mythology, pairing physical sculptures with an open digital archive and storytelling practice she names 'Re-Figuring' — coining and theorizing 'digital colonialism' as a critical framework for whose myths and data get to persist.

works.title: Source.Plus / PD12M
works.artist: Spawning (Mat Dryhurst, Holly Herndon, Jordan Meyer)
works.venue_prize: Spawning (source.plus)
works.decisive_move: Builds the rights-clear alternative to indiscriminate scraping: public-domain/CC0 images run through nudity, gore and PII classifiers and auto-recaptioned into PD12M, a 12.4-million-pair dataset — provenance-as-infrastructure, don't just flag stolen data, manufacture the clean substitute at training scale.

works.title: Starmirror
works.venue_prize: KW Institute for Contemporary Art, Berlin, with Kunstsammlung NRW (K21), Düsseldorf
works.decisive_move: Trains a new call-and-response choral dataset live from visitors, on selected Sundays, using Hildegard von Bingen's 12th-century 'Ordo Virtutum' as source text, and pairs it with 'Public Diffusion' — an image model trained entirely on public-domain data — making squeaky-clean provenance the explicit premise of the whole installation.

works.title: Synthetic Memories
works.venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2025 — Ars Electronica Award for Digital Humanity
works.decisive_move: Reconstructs fading or traumatic personal memories of elderly, dementia-affected, migrant and marginalized people into printed images via generative AI refined through live interviewee feedback loops.

works.title: The Call
works.venue_prize: Serpentine North, London (Serpentine Arts Technologies)
works.decisive_move: Recorded 15 UK community choirs under a purpose-written songbook/recording protocol, then placed the resulting voice dataset under a 'Data Trust' governance experiment (a Trusted Data Intermediary distributing power between contributors and model-users) before using it to train the choral AI models that fill the chapel-like installation — consent becomes both method and subject, not backstory.

works.title: The Island
works.venue_prize: Osservatorio Fondazione Prada, Milan
works.decisive_move: Stages an actor playing Flash Gordon 'appearing' on the real Croatian island of Korčula to save the world, threading documentary and fiction through sculptural projection-spheres — using 'junk time' vs. 'deep time' to interrogate what counts as real, staged, or generated footage in a media environment already saturated with synthetic images.

works.title: xhairymutantx
works.venue_prize: Whitney Biennial 2024 ('Even Better Than the Real Thing'), Whitney Museum of American Art, New York
works.decisive_move: Trains a public text-to-image model on costume-distorted images of the artist's own body and invites users to generate ever-more 'hairy mutant' variants — a deliberate data-poisoning campaign against her own likeness, engineered so future scrapes of 'Holly Herndon' degrade the identity chain instead of reproducing it.

works.title: 6,500 Alphabets Make a Map
works.venue_prize: transmediale 2026 – By the Mango Belt & Tamarind Road, Kuppelhalle, silent green Kulturquartier, Berlin
works.decisive_move: Large-scale dome installation built from 6,500 digitized African pictographs, syllabaries and ideographs (extending the artist's 2019–2023 'Ancestral Algorithm' project) that reframes 'algorithm' as 'algo + rhythm', proposing indigenous knowledge systems as an alternative computational epistemics rather than an ornament to it.

works.title: Ambiogenesis
works.venue_prize: HEK Basel — "Other Intelligences" (May 10–Aug 10, 2025)
works.decisive_move: AI-voiced aquatic organisms narrate a post-human ecosystem with a 'radically different understanding of life and temporality,' using generative voice synthesis to give speculative, non-human timescales a first-person register.

works.title: Balot NFT / White Cube Lusanga
works.artist: CATPC (Congolese Plantation Workers Art League) with Human Activities / Renzo Martens
works.venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — "Fellow Travellers" (Sept 21, 2024–2025); also S+T+ARTS Prize Africa '24 Grand Prize
works.decisive_move: Congolese ex-plantation workers mint NFTs of a contested 1931 anti-colonial sculpture ("Balot") held by a Virginia museum, using the sale proceeds to literally buy back plantation land — turning blockchain provenance into a restitution and land-reclamation instrument instead of a speculative art-market gimmick.

works.title: Conversations with Bina48
works.venue_prize: Whitney Museum of American Art (acquired Fragments 7, 6, 5, 2, 2019)
works.decisive_move: Documents a years-long, ongoing videotaped dialogue between the artist and Bina48 — a social robot modeled on a Black woman but never programmed with Black history or identity — exposing the racial blind spots baked into 'universal' AI personhood.

works.title: Cybernetics, or Ghosts?
works.artist: Michael Salu (editor) & James Ginzburg (music), with 15 international writers
works.venue_prize: CTM Festival 2025 – Stranger AI, radialsystem, Berlin (28 Jan 2025)
works.decisive_move: Fifteen writers respond in fiction to Italo Calvino's 1967 essay on cybernetics and myth, paired with a companion album, using the generative-AI moment as a mirror held up to older mythic and oral world-building traditions.

works.title: Data Justice Program & Digital Stewards
works.year: 2012–ongoing (Digital Stewards from 2012; Data Justice Program from 2016)
works.venue_prize: Detroit Community Technology Project / Detroit Digital Justice Coalition
works.decisive_move: Trains neighborhood residents as 'Digital Stewards' who physically build community-owned mesh Wi-Fi networks in Detroit neighborhoods, then extends that same infrastructural-sovereignty logic to the city's Open Data portal — resisting extraction by building parallel, community-held technical infrastructure rather than only critiquing the existing one.

works.title: Indigenous Protocol and Artificial Intelligence Position Paper
works.artist: Jason Edward Lewis et al. (Indigenous Protocol and AI Working Group)
works.venue_prize: Indigenous Protocol and Artificial Intelligence Working Group / Concordia University (Spectrum repository)
works.decisive_move: Convenes Indigenous artists, scholars and technologists to draft founding ethical guidelines asking what AI would look like if it emerged from Indigenous rather than Western epistemologies — the canon document that seeded the later Abundant Intelligences research program.

works.title: Indigenous Protocols for AI — Gallery 3
works.artist: Angie Abdilla / Old Ways, New (with Jason Edward Lewis, Oiwi Parker Jones)
works.venue_prize: Old Ways, New — Gallery 3 (online); ANAT workshop series
works.decisive_move: Develops Indigenous-authored governance protocols ('Country Centred Design') for AI systems through cross-continental workshops (Aotearoa, Australia, North America, Pacific), presented as a public gallery of cultural output rather than a conventional single artwork — positioning First Nations peoples as authors of AI ethics, not subjects of it.

works.title: MEMORY (Drawing Operations Unit: Generation 2 / D.O.U.G._2)
works.venue_prize: Victoria and Albert Museum, London (collection acquisition, 2022)
works.decisive_move: Performs live collaborative drawing alongside a robotic arm driven by a recurrent neural network trained on two decades of her own gestures; the V&A later acquires the trained model itself — sealed in a slowly degrading 3D-printed resin case — the first AI model collected as an artwork by a major museum, staging human/machine co-authorship as more-than-human kinship rather than tool-use.

works.title: Mapu Kufüll (Land Seafood)
works.venue_prize: Museo del Hongo / FICWallmapu; touring screen program
works.decisive_move: Uses video-game-style 3D animation to follow a child gathering wild mushrooms under a grandmother's guidance, reclaiming fungi (mapu kufüll) — a food source that helped Mapuche communities survive displacement during the Occupation of Araucanía — as more-than-human ally and symbol of resistance, told through Indigenous rather than Western bio-art framing.

works.title: Masakhane
works.venue_prize: Founded at Deep Learning Indaba 2019; featured at Ars Electronica ('Who Owns the Truth')
works.decisive_move: A distributed, no-prerequisite community ('we build together' in isiZulu) crowdsources open datasets, translation and speech models for African languages historically excluded from big tech's language models — treating linguistic decolonization as ongoing collective infrastructure-building rather than a single authored work.

works.title: Matrix Vegetal
works.venue_prize: HEK Basel — "Other Intelligences" (May 10–Aug 10, 2025)
works.decisive_move: A video installation braids Peruvian plant-healing practices together with 'quantum-inspired' technology, treating indeterminacy and non-Western epistemics of plant communication as equally valid registers of knowledge alongside computation.

works.title: Night Fishing with Ancestors
works.venue_prize: Secession, Vienna (2023); also Mori Art Museum Tokyo, Goldsmiths CCA London
works.decisive_move: An Indigenous-run collective of roughly 30 Belyuen community members re-stages 18th-century Macassan–Aboriginal trade history using iPhone-shot 'improvisational realism,' layering and superimposing footage to collapse colonial linear time into simultaneous ancestral and contemporary registers.

works.title: Nosukaay
works.venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2024 — Interactive Art+ (Golden Nica)
works.decisive_move: A hand-woven West African (Manjak) loincloth functions as a literal keyboard/interface, arguing that weaving and computing share a common ancestral logic — an alternative, non-Western origin story for the machine.

works.title: Not the Only One (N'TOO)
works.venue_prize: Museum of Contemporary Photography Chicago; Seattle Art Fair; Carnegie Mellon University
works.decisive_move: Trains a sculptural voice-AI oracle exclusively on oral histories from three generations of the artist's own Black family, refusing commercial datasets she considers irreparably biased — the AI's glitches and refusals become deliberate pedagogy about data sovereignty rather than bugs to be fixed.

works.title: Our Data Bodies / Digital Defense Playbook
works.artist: Our Data Bodies Project (Detroit Community Technology Project, Stop LAPD Spying Coalition, Center for Community Transitions)
works.venue_prize: Allied Media Projects (publisher); odbproject.org
works.decisive_move: Runs two years of workshops in Detroit, Charlotte and LA neighborhoods so residents can map how re-entry, housing and welfare data systems police them, then distills the findings into a free community 'Digital Defense Playbook' of counter-surveillance tools instead of an academic report.

works.title: PL'AI
works.venue_prize: HEK Basel — "Other Intelligences" (May 10–Aug 10, 2025)
works.decisive_move: An AI-controlled robot 'plays' with live cucumber plants in a terrarium via Lidar scanning and colored-ball placement, forcing a literal, physical protocol of interspecies communication between machine intelligence and plant behavior rather than a metaphorical one.

works.title: Phyto-Travellers
works.venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — ZKM_Kubus Subraum, part of "Fellow Travellers" (July 26, 2025–Feb 22, 2026)
works.decisive_move: A living garden of colonial-era "neophyte" plants is grown on freight pallets inside a 2:3-scale reconstruction of Columbus's ship the Niña, with the plants' original indigenous names inscribed on the glass façade — a literal, growing archive of botanical colonialism rather than a data visualization of it.

works.title: Pȟehíŋ kiŋ líla akhíšoke (Her hair was heavy)
works.venue_prize: SAW Video / Knot Project Space; artist portfolio
works.decisive_move: Performs wearing a braided-hair interface woven with sensors and machine learning that 'listens without ears,' translating touch and movement into light, sound and AI-generated text — staging the technology as a spirit-greeting device within Lakȟóta protocol rather than a neutral tool.

works.title: Repellent Fence
works.venue_prize: US/Mexico border, Douglas AZ–Agua Prieta MX (four-day land art action)
works.decisive_move: Floats 26 ten-foot 'scare-eye' balloons, decorated with Indigenous medicine colors, in a two-mile line straddling the US/Mexico border — a temporary, four-day land-art 'suture' re-joining Indigenous land that a colonial border fence had divided.

works.title: SummitShare — Open Digital Infrastructure for Shared Histories (Cultural Heritage)
works.artist: SummitShare / Shared Histories Team (Mulenga Kapwepwe, Thomas Gondwe, Nhyira Amofa-Sekyi, Mario Jere)
works.venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Golden Nica)
works.decisive_move: A blockchain-based open-access virtual museum performing 'metadata healing' on colonial-era Zambian artifacts, centering indigenous women custodians and routing financial support back to heritage communities.

works.title: The Open Boat
works.venue_prize: ZKM Karlsruhe — "Fellow Travellers" opening performance, Sept 20, 2024
works.decisive_move: A sound/performance piece built from postcolonial theory and the non-linear, cyclical temporalities of oceanic space, staging Black Atlantic migration and diaspora as a counter-epistemology to Western linear data and archival logic.

works.title: The Planetary Compendium
works.venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Honorary Mention)
works.decisive_move: A living, continuously evolving digital atlas of essays and case studies mapping planetary-governance frameworks, built as a scalable educational tool rather than a fixed artwork.

works.title: overturns
works.venue_prize: Swiss Institute, New York (first US institutional solo exhibition)
works.decisive_move: Reworks Sylvia Wynter's call to overturn Western 'genres of the human' into a suite of diagrams, models and sculptural systems mapping planetary racial capital against co-existing Black and Indigenous cosmologies — treating decolonization as a systems/data problem, not a site-specific one.

works.title: AI, Ain't I a Woman?
works.venue_prize: Algorithmic Justice League / MIT
works.decisive_move: A spoken-word video poem restages Sojourner Truth's 1851 question through five commercial facial-analysis APIs (Microsoft, IBM, Amazon, Face++, Google) misgendering Oprah, Serena Williams, Michelle Obama and Shirley Chisholm on screen — turns benchmark error rates into a recited indictment.

works.title: Algorithmic Justice League
works.venue_prize: ajl.org (nonprofit, est. 2016)
works.decisive_move: Institutionalizes 'the coded gaze' as a named, citable object of critique — fuses art, research and policy testimony into a standing instrument that keeps auditing deployed AI systems rather than resting on a single artwork.

works.title: Coded Bias
works.artist: Shalini Kantayya (director), featuring Joy Buolamwini, Cathy O'Neil, Zeynep Tufekci, Safiya Noble, Deborah Raji
works.venue_prize: Sundance Film Festival 2020; Netflix / PBS Independent Lens
works.decisive_move: Documents the Gender Shades audit escalating into congressional testimony and municipal facial-recognition bans, narrativizing an algorithmic accuracy study as a feminist accountability thriller.

works.title: Data Feminism
works.venue_prize: MIT Press (open access edition)
works.decisive_move: Reframes data science through intersectional feminist theory, arguing that counting and classification themselves encode power — turns 'whose voice is missing from the data' into a repeatable analytic method rather than a moral aside.

works.title: Dear Data
works.venue_prize: MoMA permanent collection (postcards + sketchbooks)
works.decisive_move: Two designers mail each other one hand-drawn postcard a week for a year, each encoding a mundane personal metric (complaints, doors touched, laughter) — asserts 'data humanism': the antidote to extractive big data is slow, subjective, handmade small data shared as intimacy.

works.title: Feminist Data Set
works.venue_prize: Ars Electronica, LABoral, Victoria and Albert Museum, MAMbo Bologna, SOHO20 Gallery
works.decisive_move: Interrogates every stage of a machine-learning pipeline — collection, labeling, training, algorithm choice, chatbot design — through participatory feminist workshops, treating deliberately slow, intentional data-gathering itself as an act of protest against extractive data culture.

works.title: Gender Shades
works.venue_prize: MIT Media Lab; published at Conference on Fairness, Accountability and Transparency (FAccT)
works.decisive_move: Builds an intersectional benchmark (Pilot Parliaments Benchmark, 1,270 faces) to show commercial gender-classifiers erring up to 34.7% on darker-skinned women versus 0.8% on lighter-skinned men — an academic audit engineered as public evidence, which pushed IBM to end facial-recognition sales.

works.title: Harvesting Climate Action
works.venue_prize: Prix Ars Electronica 2026 — Digital Humanity (Honorary Mention)
works.decisive_move: Combines digital sensing tools with community culinary-identity workshops in a three-tiered (individual/group/district) structure to build climate-data awareness from the ground up.
works.curator_note: Schwacher KI-Bezug — partizipative Sensorik + Community-Workshops; als Daten-Partizipations-Werk geführt.

works.title: Missing Datasets (list/essay)
works.venue_prize: Self-published open repository, companion to the Library of Missing Datasets installation series
works.decisive_move: An open, continually-added public list plus essay cataloguing real-world datasets that go uncollected, giving the physical Library's concept a living, crowd-legible digital counterpart.

works.title: The Library of Missing Datasets
works.venue_prize: bitforms gallery (v2.0); developed during residencies at Data & Society and Eyebeam
works.decisive_move: A literal filing cabinet of empty, labeled folders — each naming a dataset that societies choose not to collect (e.g. undocumented migrants incarcerated in the US) — makes structural absence itself the exhibit.

works.title: DATALAND
works.artist: Refik Anadol Studio (Refik Anadol & Efsun Erkılıç)
works.venue_prize: The Grand LA, Los Angeles ('world's first museum of AI art')
works.decisive_move: A permanent 25,000 sq ft museum where a wristband ('Empathica') and scent-diffusing neckband feed anonymized visitor biometric/behavioral signals back into generative rainforest-data visuals — data spectacle fully institutionalized as a standing museum genre rather than a temporary blockbuster stunt.

works.title: Unsupervised
works.venue_prize: MoMA, New York (Gund Lobby; acquired into permanent collection)
works.decisive_move: Runs a generative model over 138,151 digitized works from MoMA's own collection and streams the output as a continuously mutating real-time 'hallucination' on a 24×24-ft media wall — no argument, no disclosed critique of the compute/energy behind it; over 3 million visitors, pure data-sublime as the point.
